metadata.description: Generate high-speed text-to-speech audio using ElevenLabs TTS Turbo v2.5.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/elevenlabs/tts/turbo-v2.5
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/elevenlabs/tts/turbo-v2.5 queue.

x-fal-metadata.about:
ElevenLabs Turbo v2.5 model for text-to-speech generation.

High quality with lowest latency, ideal for real-time applications.
Supports 32 languages while maintaining natural voice quality.

voice.title: Voice
voice.description: The voice to use for speech generation

previous_text.title: Previous Text
previous_text.description: The text that came before the text of the current request. Can be used to improve the speech's continuity when concatenating together multiple generations or to influence the speech's continuity in the current generation.

speed.title: Speed
speed.description: Speech speed (0.7-1.2). Values below 1.0 slow down the speech, above 1.0 speed it up. Extreme values may affect quality.

text.title: Text
examples: Hello! This is a test of the text to speech system, powered by ElevenLabs. How does it sound?
text.description: The text to convert to speech

apply_text_normalization.title: Apply Text Normalization
apply_text_normalization.description: This parameter controls text normalization with three modes: 'auto', 'on', and 'off'. When set to 'auto', the system will automatically decide whether to apply text normalization (e.g., spelling out numbers). With 'on', text normalization will always be applied, while with 'off', it will be skipped.

next_text.title: Next Text
next_text.description: The text that comes after the text of the current request. Can be used to improve the speech's continuity when concatenating together multiple generations or to influence the speech's continuity in the current generation.

timestamps.title: Timestamps
timestamps.description: Whether to return timestamps for each word in the generated speech

language_code.title: Language Code
language_code.description: Language code (ISO 639-1) used to enforce a language for the model. An error will be returned if language code is not supported by the model.

timestamps.title: Timestamps
timestamps.description: Timestamps for each word in the generated speech. Only returned if `timestamps` is set to True in the request.

file_size.title: File Size
file_size.description: The size of the file in bytes.

url.title: Url
url.description: The URL where the file can be downloaded from.

file_name.title: File Name
file_name.description: The name of the file. It will be auto-generated if not provided.

content_type.title: Content Type
content_type.description: The mime type of the file.

schema.description: Whether to include logs (`1`) in the response or not (`0`).

success.description: Whether the request was cancelled successfully.

x-fal-metadata.about: Convert text to speech using MiniMax Speech 2.8 HD - highest quality synthesis with interjection support.

prompt.description: Text to convert to speech. Use `<#x#>` for pauses (x = 0.01-99.99 seconds). Supports interjection tags: `(laughs)`, `(sighs)`, `(coughs)`, `(clears throat)`, `(gasps)`, `(sniffs)`, `(groans)`, `(yawns)`.
examples: Hello world! Welcome to MiniMax's new text to speech model <#0.1#> Speech 2.8 HD (laughs) now available on Fal!

metadata.description: Newest audio model from Google introduces granular audio tags that give you precise control to direct AI speech for expressive audio generation.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/gemini-3.1-flash-tts
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/gemini-3.1-flash-tts queue.

x-fal-metadata.about:
Generate speech from text using Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS model.

Next-generation model with improved speech quality, expressive audio
tags (e.g. [sigh], [laughing], [whispering], [short pause]), and
enhanced multi-speaker dialogue.

Gemini31FlashTtsInput.description: Input for Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS preview speech generation.
Gemini31FlashTtsInput.title: Gemini31FlashTTSInput

output_format.description: Audio output format. mp3: compressed, small file size (recommended). wav: uncompressed PCM wrapped in WAV (24 kHz, 16-bit mono). ogg_opus: Ogg container with Opus codec, good quality-to-size ratio.
output_format.title: Output Format

examples: I can't believe it worked! [laughing] After all those hours of debugging [sigh] it was just a missing semicolon.
prompt.description: The text to convert to speech. Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS supports natural-language prompting for style, pace, accent, and emotional expression — include delivery instructions inline with the text (e.g. 'Say cheerfully: Have a wonderful day!'). Supports expressive audio tags like [sigh], [laughing], [whispering], [short pause] for fine-grained control over delivery. For multi-speaker synthesis, prefix lines with speaker aliases defined in the speakers field (e.g. 'Alice: Hello!\nBob: Hi!').
prompt.title: Prompt

speakers.description: Multi-speaker voice configuration. When set, enables multi-speaker synthesis where different parts of the text are spoken by different voices. Each speaker needs a voice and a speaker_id (alias) that matches prefixes in the prompt.
speakers.title: Speakers

temperature.description: Controls the randomness of the speech output. Higher values produce more creative and varied delivery, while lower values make the output more predictable and focused.
temperature.title: Temperature

examples: Say the following in a warm, conversational tone
Read this as a dramatic newscast with gravitas
Speak with a British accent, cheerfully and energetically
style_instructions.description: Optional style and delivery instructions prepended to the prompt. Controls expressiveness, accent, pace, tone, and emotional expression using natural language. Use this to separate style control from the text content. Examples: 'Speak warmly and slowly', 'Read this as a dramatic newscast', 'Use a British accent with a cheerful tone', 'Whisper mysteriously'.
style_instructions.title: Style Instructions

language_code.description: Language for multilingual synthesis. When set, steers the model to speak in the specified language. If not set, the model auto-detects the language from the text.
language_code.title: Language Code

voice.description: Voice preset for single-speaker synthesis. 30 distinct voices are available. Ignored when speakers is set. Popular choices: Kore (strong, firm female), Puck (upbeat, lively male), Charon (calm, professional male), Zephyr (bright, clear female), Aoede (warm, melodic female).
voice.title: Voice

SpeakerConfig.description: Voice configuration for a single speaker in multi-speaker synthesis.
SpeakerConfig.title: SpeakerConfig

speaker_id.description: Alias used to identify this speaker in the prompt. Use this alias as a prefix in the prompt field, e.g. 'Alice: Hello! Bob: Hi there!'. Must be alphanumeric with no whitespace.
speaker_id.title: Speaker Id

file_size.description: The size of the file in bytes.
file_size.title: File Size

content_type.description: The mime type of the file.
content_type.title: Content Type

file_name.description: The name of the file. It will be auto-generated if not provided.
file_name.title: File Name

url.description: The URL where the file can be downloaded from.
url.title: Url

schema.description: Whether to include logs (`1`) in the response or not (`0`).

success.description: Whether the request was cancelled successfully.

metadata.display_name: Bytedance Seed Speech Text to Speech
metadata.description: Seed Speech developed by ByteDance, is a family of large-scale text-to-speech models capable of synthesizing speech that is virtually indistinguishable from human speech.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/bytedance/seed-speech/tts/v2
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/bytedance/seed-speech/tts/v2 queue.

x-fal-metadata.about: Generate speech from text using ByteDance TTS 2.0.

voice_instruction.description: Optional natural-language instruction that steers the delivery (tone, emotion, pace, volume), e.g. 'Speak in a cheerful tone' or 'Could you speak a bit slower?'. It is not spoken aloud and does not affect billing.
voice_instruction.title: Voice Instruction
examples: Speak in a warm, cheerful tone.

speed.description: Speech speed. 1.0 is normal speed, 0.5 is half speed, 2.0 is double speed.
speed.title: Speed

volume.description: Volume. 1.0 is normal volume, 0.5 is half, 2.0 is double.
volume.title: Volume

sample_rate.description: Sample rate of the output audio in Hz.
sample_rate.title: Sample Rate

output_format.description: Output audio format. 'mp3' returns MP3 audio; 'opus' returns Opus audio in an Ogg container.
output_format.title: Output Format

examples: Hello, this is a test of ByteDance TTS 2.0 speech synthesis.
text.description: The text to synthesize into speech.
text.title: Text

language.description: Force the text to be read as a single language, disabling automatic language detection. Leave unset for automatic detection (including seamless Chinese/English mixing on bilingual voices). Codes: zh (Chinese), en (English), ja (Japanese), es-mx (Mexican Spanish), id (Indonesian), pt-br (Brazilian Portuguese), ko (Korean), it (Italian), de (German), fr (French).
language.title: Language

pitch.description: Voice pitch shift in semitones. 0 is normal pitch, -12 lowers by one octave, 12 raises by one octave.
pitch.title: Pitch

voice.description: Voice to use for synthesis. The preset name encodes the voice and its supported language codes. 'mixed_en_zh' means the voice can seamlessly blend English and Chinese; separate codes (e.g. 'en_zh') mean the voice supports each language independently.
voice.title: Voice

file_size.description: The size of the file in bytes.
file_size.title: File Size

content_type.description: The mime type of the file.
content_type.title: Content Type

file_name.description: The name of the file. It will be auto-generated if not provided.
file_name.title: File Name

url.description: The URL where the file can be downloaded from.
url.title: Url

schema.description: Whether to include logs (`1`) in the response or not (`0`).

success.description: Whether the request was cancelled successfully.

metadata.description: Clone a voice from a sample audio and generate speech from text prompts using the MiniMax model, which leverages advanced AI techniques to create high-quality text-to-speech.

x-fal-metadata.about: Clone a voice from an audio URL. Optionally, generate a TTS preview with the cloned voice.

text.description: Text to generate a TTS preview with the cloned voice (optional)
examples: Hello, this is a preview of your cloned voice! I hope you like it!
text.default: Hello, this is a preview of your cloned voice! I hope you like it!
text.title: Text

audio_url.description:
URL of the input audio file for voice cloning. Should be at least 10 seconds
            long. To retain the voice permanently, use it with a TTS (text-to-speech)
            endpoint at least once within 7 days. Otherwise, it will be
            automatically deleted.
audio_url.title: Audio Url

model.description: TTS model to use for preview. Options: speech-02-hd, speech-02-turbo, speech-01-hd, speech-01-turbo
model.title: Model

noise_reduction.description: Enable noise reduction for the cloned voice
noise_reduction.title: Noise Reduction

need_volume_normalization.description: Enable volume normalization for the cloned voice
need_volume_normalization.title: Need Volume Normalization

audio.description: Preview audio generated with the cloned voice (if requested)

custom_voice_id.description: The cloned voice ID for use with TTS
custom_voice_id.title: Custom Voice Id

metadata.description: Generate speech with expressive and realistic voices from xAI

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for xai/tts/v1
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the xai/tts/v1 queue.

x-fal-metadata.about:
Convert text to speech using xAI's TTS API.

Supports 5 expressive voices, inline speech tags for fine-grained
delivery control, and output formats from high-fidelity MP3 to
telephony-optimized μ-law.

text.description: The text to convert to speech. Maximum 15,000 characters. Supports speech tags for expressive delivery: inline tags like [laugh], [pause], [sigh] and wrapping tags like <whisper>text</whisper>, <slow>text</slow>.
text.title: Text
examples: Hello! This is xAI text to speech, brought to you by Fal AI.

language.description: BCP-47 language code or 'auto' for automatic detection. Supported: en, zh, fr, de, hi, id, it, ja, ko, pt-BR, pt-PT, ru, es-MX, es-ES, tr, vi, bn, ar-EG, ar-SA, ar-AE.
language.title: Language

output_format.description: Output format configuration. Defaults to MP3 at 24 kHz / 128 kbps.

voice.description: Voice to use for synthesis. eve: energetic, upbeat. ara: warm, friendly. rex: confident, clear. sal: smooth, balanced. leo: authoritative, strong.
voice.title: Voice

OutputFormat.description: Output format configuration for the TTS API.
OutputFormat.title: OutputFormat

bit_rate.description: Bit rate in bps. Only applicable for MP3 codec. Defaults to 128000 for MP3.
bit_rate.title: Bit Rate

codec.description: Audio codec. Supported: mp3, wav, pcm, mulaw, alaw.
codec.title: Codec

sample_rate.description: Sample rate in Hz.
sample_rate.title: Sample Rate

url.description: The URL where the file can be downloaded from.
url.title: Url

content_type.description: The mime type of the file.
content_type.title: Content Type

file_size.description: The size of the file in bytes.
file_size.title: File Size

file_name.description: The name of the file. It will be auto-generated if not provided.
file_name.title: File Name

schema.description: Whether to include logs (`1`) in the response or not (`0`).

success.description: Whether the request was cancelled successfully.

metadata.description: Bring speech to your texts using Qwen3-TTS Custom-Voice model with pre-trained voices or use your custom voice with Qwen3-TTS Clone Voice model

x-fal-metadata.about: Custom Voice 17B

examples: Okay. Yeah. I resent you. I love you. I respect you. But you know what? You blew it! And it is all thanks to you.
reference_text.description: Optional reference text that was used when creating the speaker embedding. Providing this can improve synthesis quality when using a cloned voice.
reference_text.title: Reference Text

prompt.description: Optional prompt to guide the style of the generated speech. This prompt will be ignored if a speaker embedding is provided.
prompt.title: Prompt

speaker_voice_embedding_file_url.description: URL to a speaker embedding file in safetensors format, from `fal-ai/qwen-3-tts/clone-voice` endpoint. If provided, the TTS model will use the cloned voice for synthesis instead of the predefined voices.
speaker_voice_embedding_file_url.title: Speaker Voice Embedding File Url

max_new_tokens.description: Maximum number of new codec tokens to generate.
max_new_tokens.title: Max New Tokens

examples: I am solving the equation: x = [-b ± √(b²-4ac)] / 2a? Nobody can — it's a disaster (◍•͈⌔•͈◍), very sad!
text.description: The text to be converted to speech.
text.title: Text

voice.description: The voice to be used for speech synthesis, will be ignored if a speaker embedding is provided. Check out the **[documentation](https://github.com/QwenLM/Qwen3-TTS/tree/main?tab=readme-ov-file#custom-voice-generate)** for each voice's details and which language they primarily support.
voice.title: Voice

content_type.title: Content Type

metadata.description: Whether you're working on memes, videos, games, or AI agents, Chatterbox brings your content to life. Use the first tts from resemble ai.

examples: I just found a hidden treasure in the backyard! Check it out!

audio_url.title: Audio Url
audio_url.description: Optional URL to an audio file to use as a reference for the generated speech. If provided, the model will try to match the style and tone of the reference audio.

seed.title: Seed
seed.description: Useful to control the reproducibility of the generated audio. Assuming all other properties didn't change, a fixed seed should always generate the exact same audio file. Set to 0 for random seed..

exaggeration.title: Exaggeration
exaggeration.description: Exaggeration factor for the generated speech (0.0 = no exaggeration, 1.0 = maximum exaggeration).

should_use_prompt_for_emotion.description: Whether to use the `prompt` to calculate emotional strengths, if enabled it will overwrite the `emotional_strengths` values. If `emotion_prompt` is provided, it will be used to instead of `prompt` to extract the emotional style.

examples: You scared me to death! What are you, a ghost?

prompt.description: Text to convert to speech. Paragraph breaks should be marked with newline characters. **NOTE**: You can customize speech pauses by adding markers in the form `<#x#>`, where `x` is the pause duration in seconds. Valid range: `[0.01, 99.99]`, up to two decimal places. Pause markers must be placed between speakable text segments and cannot be used consecutively.
examples: Hello world! Welcome MiniMax's new text to speech model <#0.1#> Speech 2.6 Turbo, now available on Fal!

Design a personalized voice from a text description.

This API supports generating personalized voices based on user-input voice description prompts,
and supports using the generated voice (voice_id) for speech generation in Text to Speech API.

examples: Bubbly and excitable female pop star interviewee, youthful, slightly breathless, and very enthusiastic

examples: Oh my gosh, hi. It's like so amazing to be here. This new endpoint just dropped on fal and the results have been like totally incredible. Use it now, It's gonna be like epic!

metadata.description: Maya1 is a state-of-the-art speech model by Maya Research for expressive voice generation, built to capture real human emotion and precise voice design.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/maya
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/maya queue.

x-fal-metadata.about: Generate speech from text using Maya-1-Voice

prompt.description: Description of the voice/character. Includes attributes like age, accent, pitch, timbre, pacing, tone, and intensity. See examples for format.
examples: Realistic male voice in the 30s age with american accent. Normal pitch, warm timbre, conversational pacing, neutral tone delivery at med intensity.
Creative, dark_villain character. Male voice in their 40s with british accent. Low pitch, gravelly timbre, slow pacing, angry tone at high intensity.
prompt.title: Prompt

output_format.description: Output audio format for the generated speech
output_format.title: Output Format

repetition_penalty.description: Penalty for repeating tokens. Higher values reduce repetition artifacts.
repetition_penalty.title: Repetition Penalty

temperature.description: Sampling temperature. Lower values (0.2-0.5) produce more stable/consistent audio. Higher values add variation.
temperature.title: Temperature

text.description: The text to synthesize into speech. You can embed emotion tags anywhere in the text using the format <emotion_name>. Available emotions: laugh, laugh_harder, sigh, chuckle, gasp, angry, excited, whisper, cry, scream, sing, snort, exhale, gulp, giggle, sarcastic, curious. Example: 'Hello world! <excited> This is amazing!' or 'I can't believe this <sigh> happened again.'
examples: Hello world! This is a test of the Maya-1-Voice text-to-speech system.
The darkness isn't coming... <angry> it's already here!
That's hilarious! <laugh> I can't stop <laugh_harder> thinking about it!
<whisper> I have a secret to tell you. <excited> You won't believe what happened!
text.title: Text

top_p.description: Nucleus sampling parameter. Controls diversity of token selection.
top_p.title: Top P

sample_rate.description: Output audio sample rate. 48 kHz provides higher quality audio, 24 kHz is faster.
sample_rate.title: Sample Rate

max_tokens.description: Maximum number of SNAC tokens to generate (7 tokens per frame). Controls maximum audio length.
max_tokens.title: Max Tokens

MayaInput.title: MayaVoiceInput

rtf.title: Rtf

sample_rate.title: Sample Rate

duration.title: Duration

text.description: The text to be converted to speech (maximum 300 characters). Supports 23 languages including English, French, German, Spanish, Italian, Portuguese, Hindi, Arabic, Chinese, Japanese, Korean, and more.
examples: Last month, we reached a new milestone with two billion views on our YouTube channel.
Le mois dernier, nous avons atteint un nouveau jalon avec deux milliards de vues sur notre chaîne YouTube.

cfg_scale.title: CFG Scale
cfg_scale.description: Configuration/pace weight controlling generation guidance (0.0-1.0). Use 0.0 for language transfer to mitigate accent inheritance.

custom_audio_language.title: Custom Audio Language
custom_audio_language.description: If using a custom audio URL, specify the language of the audio here. Ignored if voice is not a custom url.

temperature.title: Temperature
temperature.description: Controls randomness and variation in generation (0.05-5.0). Higher values create more varied speech patterns.

seed.title: Seed
seed.description: Random seed for reproducible results. Set to 0 for random generation, or provide a specific number for consistent outputs.

exaggeration.title: Exaggeration
exaggeration.description: Controls speech expressiveness and emotional intensity (0.25-2.0). 0.5 is neutral, higher values increase expressiveness. Extreme values may be unstable.

metadata.description: Maya1 is a state-of-the-art speech model by Maya Research for expressive voice generation, built to capture real human emotion and precise voice design.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/maya/stream
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/maya/stream queue.

x-fal-metadata.about:
Generate speech from text with streaming audio output.

Returns streaming audio chunks as they are generated.
Uses VLLM AsyncLLMEngine for token-by-token streaming.

Supports multiple output formats:
- 'mp3': Browser-playable compressed audio (default, recommended)
- 'wav': Uncompressed audio with headers
- 'pcm': Raw PCM (lowest latency, requires client-side decoding)

Supports sample rates:
- '24 kHz': Native SNAC output (default, faster, lower latency)
- '48 kHz': Upsampled audio (higher quality, more latency)

prompt.description: Description of the voice/character. Includes attributes like age, accent, pitch, timbre, pacing, tone, and intensity. See examples for format.
examples: Realistic male voice in the 30s age with american accent. Normal pitch, warm timbre, conversational pacing, neutral tone delivery at med intensity.
Creative, dark_villain character. Male voice in their 40s with british accent. Low pitch, gravelly timbre, slow pacing, angry tone at high intensity.
prompt.title: Prompt

output_format.description: Output audio format. 'mp3' for browser-playable audio, 'wav' for uncompressed audio, 'pcm' for raw PCM (lowest latency, requires client-side decoding).
output_format.title: Output Format

repetition_penalty.description: Penalty for repeating tokens. Higher values reduce repetition artifacts.
repetition_penalty.title: Repetition Penalty

temperature.description: Sampling temperature. Lower values (0.2-0.5) produce more stable/consistent audio. Higher values add variation.
temperature.title: Temperature

text.description: The text to synthesize into speech. You can embed emotion tags anywhere in the text using the format <emotion_name>. Available emotions: laugh, laugh_harder, sigh, chuckle, gasp, angry, excited, whisper, cry, scream, sing, snort, exhale, gulp, giggle, sarcastic, curious. Example: 'Hello world! <excited> This is amazing!' or 'I can't believe this <sigh> happened again.'
examples: Hello world! This is a test of the Maya-1-Voice text-to-speech system.
The darkness isn't coming... <angry> it's already here!
text.title: Text

top_p.description: Nucleus sampling parameter. Controls diversity of token selection.
top_p.title: Top P

sample_rate.description: Output audio sample rate. 48 kHz uses upsampling for higher quality audio, 24 kHz is native SNAC output (faster, lower latency).
sample_rate.title: Sample Rate

max_tokens.description: Maximum number of SNAC tokens to generate (7 tokens per frame). Controls maximum audio length.
max_tokens.title: Max Tokens

MayaStreamInput.title: MayaVoiceStreamingInput

metadata.description: Generate expressive, natural speech with Resemble AI's Chatterbox. Features unique emotion control, instant voice cloning from short audio, and built-in watermarking.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for resemble-ai/chatterboxhd/text-to-speech
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the resemble-ai/chatterboxhd/text-to-speech queue.

x-fal-metadata.about:
This endpoint generates a TTS audio file from a given text input.

Fal.ai injects an environment variable FAL_REQUEST_ID for each request.
This allows us to correlate logs belonging to a specific API call in the Fal.ai dashboard.
We use "local" as a default if the variable isn't set (e.g., when running locally
without fal run in a way that injects it, though fal run usually handles this).
The request_id is prepended to log messages within the endpoint handlers
(e.g., logger.info(f"[{request_id}] ...")).

Parameters:
request: TTSInput - The input parameters for the TTS request.
response: Response - The response object for the TTS request.

Returns:
TTSOutput - The generated audio file and metadata.

ChatterboxhdTextToSpeechInput.description: Input parameters for the TTS request.

text.description: Text to synthesize into speech.
text.title: Text
text.default: My name is Maximus Decimus Meridius, commander of the Armies of the North, General of the Felix Legions and loyal servant to the true emperor, Marcus Aurelius. Father to a murdered son, husband to a murdered wife. And I will have my vengeance, in this life or the next.

exaggeration.description: Controls emotion exaggeration. Range typically 0.25 to 2.0.
exaggeration.title: Exaggeration

high_quality_audio.description: If True, the generated audio will be upscaled to 48kHz. The generation of the audio will take longer, but the quality will be higher. If False, the generated audio will be 24kHz.
high_quality_audio.title: High Quality Audio

voice.description: The voice to use for the TTS request. If neither voice nor audio are provided, a random voice will be used.
voice.title: Voice

audio_url.description: URL to the audio sample to use as a voice prompt for zero-shot TTS voice cloning. Providing a audio sample will override the voice setting. If neither voice nor audio_url are provided, a random voice will be used.
audio_url.title: Audio Url

temperature.description: Controls the randomness of generation. Range typically 0.05 to 5.
temperature.title: Temperature

seed.description: Useful to control the reproducibility of the generated audio. Assuming all other properties didn't change, a fixed seed should always generate the exact same audio file. Set to 0 for random seed.
seed.title: Seed

cfg.description: Classifier-free guidance scale (CFG) controls the conditioning factor. Range typically 0.2 to 1.0. For expressive or dramatic speech, try lower cfg values (e.g. ~0.3) and increase exaggeration to around 0.7 or higher. If the reference speaker has a fast speaking style, lowering cfg to around 0.3 can improve pacing.
cfg.title: Cfg

ChatterboxhdTextToSpeechInput.title: TTSInput

ChatterboxhdTextToSpeechOutput.description: Output parameters for the TTS request.
ChatterboxhdTextToSpeechOutput.title: TTSOutput

file_size.description: The size of the file in bytes.
file_size.title: File Size

file_name.description: The name of the file. It will be auto-generated if not provided.
file_name.title: File Name

content_type.description: The mime type of the file.
content_type.title: Content Type

url.description: The URL where the file can be downloaded from.
url.title: Url

schema.description: Whether to include logs (`1`) in the response or not (`0`).

success.description: Whether the request was cancelled successfully.
